nits.issue: DELETE on an already-archived automation returns 200 + {idempotent: true} instead of 404. The verify contract only requires 'GET-after-delete returns 404' (which holds), so this isn't a violation, but a second DELETE diverging from REST-typical 404 is worth a comment for API consumers.
GET /:id/versions has no pagination/limit; for automations with many versions this could return a large payload.
publicShape() is just a cast-through; either drop or use it to strip workspace_id.
Trigger union: a payload with both `cron` and `at` would silently parse as RecurringScheduleTrigger. A Zod `.strict()` or discriminated refine would catch user mistakes earlier.
Pattern `${JSON.stringify(v)}::jsonb` matches the established cloud-runs/approvals usage. Memory note prefers `sql.json(v)` shape — worth standardizing across the codebase.
